title: Biology for Mortals: Exploring Nature's Hidden Code
author: Wordsus Team
description: Explore the mysteries of life through 365 fascinating lessons. From DNA and genetics to evolution and deep ecology, explained simply for everyone.

chapters.title: What is life? Definition and key characteristics
chapters.description: Discover the fundamental biological concepts that separate living beings from inert matter and how science defines the beginning of life.

chapters.title: DNA revealed: The great molecule of heredity
chapters.description: We explore the structure of deoxyribonucleic acid, its famous double helix design, and how it stores our most essential genetic information.

chapters.title: The eukaryotic cell: Its structure and functions
chapters.description: We travel inside our own cells to learn about their organelles, their vital functions and how they cooperate to keep us alive every day.

chapters.title: Biological evolution: The mechanism of adaptation
chapters.description: We analyze the foundations of natural selection proposed by Darwin and how organisms slowly change over geological eras.

chapters.title: The Kingdom of Bacteria: Ubiquitous Microorganisms
chapters.description: Learn about the fascinating world of bacteria, the most abundant living beings on the planet, and understand their vital importance for all ecosystems.

chapters.title: Are viruses live? Your particular biological cycle
chapters.description: We discuss the true biological nature of viruses, their methods of invading host cells, and how they manage to multiply so effectively.

chapters.title: Fungi: The fascinating kingdom of fungi and yeasts
chapters.description: We discover the biology of fungi, from single-celled yeasts to the complex networks of mycelium that connect large natural forests.

chapters.title: The genetics of eye color in humans
chapters.description: We break down how our inherited genes interact to determine the amount of melanin in the iris and generate the different colors of our eyes.

chapters.title: Photosynthesis: How plants make their food
chapters.description: We study the biochemical process by which plants capture sunlight and carbon dioxide to produce their own energy and oxygen.

chapters.title: Animal migration: Biology of great journeys
chapters.description: We explore the incredible biological and physiological mechanisms that allow diverse species to navigate and survive journeys of thousands of kilometers.

chapters.title: Human blood groups and their hereditary basis
chapters.description: Understand the genetic difference between ABO system blood groups and the Rh factor, and why this compatibility is crucial in today's medicine.

chapters.title: The immune system: The body's defenses
chapters.description: We analyze the cells and processes that make up our immune system, which protects us from pathogens and diseases in a constant and silent way.

chapters.title: The biology of sleep: Why we sleep and its phases
chapters.description: We review neural processes, circadian rhythms, and the great biological importance of sleeping well to maintain optimal physical and mental health.

chapters.title: The process of digestion and absorption of nutrients
chapters.description: We accompany food on its journey through the human digestive system to understand how we extract and assimilate the energy necessary to live.

chapters.title: CRISPR-Cas9: The revolution in gene editing
chapters.description: Learn about the genetic editing tool inspired by the bacterial immune system that allows scientists to modify DNA sequences with precision.

chapters.title: Metamorphosis: Extreme biological transformations
chapters.description: Discover the impressive morphological and physiological changes that insects and amphibians go through during their deep development until the adult phase.

chapters.title: The biology of human aging at the cellular level
chapters.description: We analyze telomere shortening and oxidative stress to understand the cellular and genetic factors that cause our aging.

chapters.title: The biological bases of memory and learning
chapters.description: We explore how our neurons create new synaptic connections in the brain to process, store and retrieve the information we learn.

chapters.title: Symbiosis: Cooperation between different species
chapters.description: We study the fascinating symbiotic relationships, where different organisms evolve together to benefit each other and survive.

chapters.title: Extremophiles: Life in the most hostile environments
chapters.description: Meet the amazing microorganisms genetically and biologically adapted to thrive in conditions of extreme heat, high acidity or radiation.

chapters.title: Hominid Evolution: Our Human Lineage
chapters.description: We trace the long evolutionary journey from our oldest primate ancestors to the consolidation of modern Homo sapiens on planet Earth.

chapters.title: Feathered dinosaurs and the origin of birds
chapters.description: We examine the compelling biological and paleontological evidence showing how modern birds descended directly from theropod dinosaurs.

chapters.title: Epigenetics: How the environment influences genes
chapters.description: Discover how certain external and environmental factors manage to activate or deactivate our genes without altering the fundamental sequence of our human DNA.

chapters.title: The human microbiome: We are a walking ecosystem
chapters.description: We analyze the billions of microorganisms that live on our skin and inside the intestine, and their crucial biological impact on our general health.

chapters.title: Human stem cells: Potential and development
chapters.description: We explain what exactly stem cells are, their incredible ability to specialize in different tissues and their enormous potential in current medicine.

chapters.title: Bioluminescence: Animals that produce their own light
chapters.description: We study the chemical processes that allow certain jellyfish, insects and abyssal fish to generate their own light to hunt, communicate or defend themselves.

chapters.title: Biology of allergies: The active immune system
chapters.description: Understand what happens biologically in our body when the immune system overreacts and unnecessarily to harmless substances.

chapters.title: Adenosine triphosphate: The currency of energy
chapters.description: We delve into cellular biochemistry to understand the function of the ATP molecule, responsible for storing and transferring vital energy in living beings.

chapters.title: Genetic mutations: Errors that bring change
chapters.description: We clarify how spontaneous changes occur in our genetic sequence, causing hereditary diseases or driving natural evolutionary diversity.

chapters.title: The Biology of Twins: Shared Identity
chapters.description: We differentiate biological formation between identical and fraternal twins, exploring how they share genetic information in early development.

chapters.title: Biology of pollination and plant reproduction
chapters.description: We analyze how flowering plants manage to reproduce thanks to the crucial intervention of pollinating insects and other key environmental factors.

chapters.title: Neanderthal DNA in modern humans today
chapters.description: Discover the fascinating genetic evidence that shows how our ancestors interbred with Neanderthals and what genes we inherited from them.

chapters.title: Physiology of the cardiovascular system of humans
chapters.description: We analyze the impressive biology of the heart and blood vessels, understanding how they pump blood to nourish each and every cell.

chapters.title: Apoptosis: Programmed cell death and its function
chapters.description: We understand the essential biological mechanism by which our cells decide to self-destruct in an orderly manner to protect the health of the entire organism.

chapters.title: The biology of dopamine and the reward system
chapters.description: We explore the neurobiology behind pleasure and motivation, unraveling the fundamental role of neurotransmitters in our daily behaviors.

chapters.title: Food webs: The flow of energy in ecology
chapters.description: We explain the biological dynamics that connect producer, consumer and decomposer organisms, maintaining the great ecosystem balance.

chapters.title: Poisonous and poisonous animals: Biological differences
chapters.description: We differentiate the complex evolutionary mechanisms that animals use to inject or secrete lethal toxins as a method of hunting or survival.

chapters.title: Genetic cloning: History, science and its limits
chapters.description: We review the amazing biology behind mammal cloning, from the historic case of Dolly the sheep to modern genetic applications.

chapters.title: Homeostasis: The internal balance of living beings
chapters.description: Discover how living organisms manage to actively regulate their temperature, pH and other internal biological factors in the face of strong external changes.

chapters.title: The surprising defense strategies of plants
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating chemical and structural adaptations that wild flora evolutionarily developed to protect themselves from multiple herbivores.

chapters.title: Bacteriophages: Biological viruses that hunt bacteria
chapters.description: Delve into the biology of phages, the most lethal entities on the planet that exclusively infect bacteria and revolutionize genetic therapies.

chapters.title: The human endocrine system and the power of hormones
chapters.description: We look at how the body uses complex chemical signals in the bloodstream to regulate growth, our metabolism and reproduction.

chapters.title: Meiosis: The key cell division for reproduction
chapters.description: We understand the meticulous biological process that halves our chromosomes to form eggs and sperm, guaranteeing variability.

chapters.title: Convergent evolution: When biology repeats designs
chapters.description: We discover why species from completely different lineages end up developing similar anatomical traits to face the same environmental challenges.

chapters.title: The carbon cycle: Chemical engine of the entire biosphere
chapters.description: We trace the amazing journey of carbon atoms through our atmosphere, land and the living things that support ecosystem life.

chapters.title: Forensic genetics and reading the DNA fingerprint
chapters.description: We explain in biological detail how specialists analyze fragments of genetic material to identify individuals with great precision.

chapters.title: Biological enzymes: The body's great engines
chapters.description: We explore the crucial physiological function of these sophisticated proteins responsible for accelerating each and every one of our vital chemical reactions.

chapters.title: The complex biology of the eye and the wonder of vision
chapters.description: We break down human ocular anatomy to understand how we capture light from the environment and transform it into clear neurobiological electrical impulses.

chapters.title: Carnivorous plants: Surprising biological adaptations
chapters.description: Delve into the fascinating evolution of those plants that inhabit soils lacking nutrients and have learned to capture and devour small insects.

chapters.title: Plasma membrane: The frontier of human cells
chapters.description: We analyze the dynamic and vital lipid barrier that surrounds our cells, deciding exactly which molecules can enter and which must leave.

chapters.title: Echolocation: The acoustic perception of nature
chapters.description: Understand the amazing anatomical biology behind the natural sonar that bats and marine cetaceans use to hunt and navigate in the dark.

chapters.title: The neurobiology of dreams: Why the mind creates
chapters.description: We explore what exactly happens in our complex brain circuits during REM sleep and what the biological theories are about it.

chapters.title: Invasive species and their devastating biological impact
chapters.description: We analyze the serious ecological consequences that occur when a foreign organism is accidentally introduced into an unprepared ecosystem.

chapters.title: LUCA: The fascinating common ancestor of all current life
chapters.description: We go back billions of years in biological evolution to investigate the characteristics of the first cellular population on our planet.

chapters.title: The Biology of Metabolism: Genetics of Body Weight
chapters.description: We demystify how our hereditary genes and biochemical environment truly influence energy control and our actual physical weight.

chapters.title: Coral reefs: The great cities of the oceans
chapters.description: Immerse yourself in the amazing biology of these marine cnidarians that form complex calcareous structures that support the rich oceanic biodiversity.

chapters.title: Hemoglobin and the miracle of oxygen transport
chapters.description: We study in detail the valuable protein in our red blood cells, their amazing chemical affinity for gases and their enormous role in survival.

chapters.title: Neuroplasticity: The human brain that always shapes itself
chapters.description: Discover how neuronal biology allows us to constantly readapt our cognitive pathways in the face of learning, new experiences and inclement conditions.

chapters.title: Biological parasitism and host manipulation
chapters.description: Delve into the disturbing adaptive strategies of organisms that invade other living beings to snatch nutrients and control behaviors.

chapters.title: The lymphatic system: The cleansing network of the human body
chapters.description: We analyze this silent network of ducts parallel to the circulatory system, biologically essential to regulate fluids and maintain immune defense.

chapters.title: Pheromones and the biology of sexual attraction
chapters.description: We explore the complex, invisible chemical signals that many animals and perhaps we use to communicate and attract our mates.

chapters.title: Mitosis: How our cells manage to multiply
chapters.description: We analyze the fundamental biological process of cell division that allows us to grow from a single zygote and constantly repair our tissues.

chapters.title: The biology of hibernation and animal torpor
chapters.description: Discover the amazing metabolic and physiological changes that allow various species to survive the harshest winters without tasting food.

chapters.title: Messenger RNA: The great translator of genetics
chapters.description: We study the vital molecule that copies information from nuclear DNA and transports it to make the proteins essential for our own life.

chapters.title: Biology of the muscular system and physical movement
chapters.description: We break down the fascinating biochemical interaction between actin and myosin that makes it possible for our muscles to contract so we can move.

chapters.title: Cancer biology: When cells rebel
chapters.description: We understand the genetic and molecular errors that cause our own cells to lose control and begin to divide chaotically.

chapters.title: Eusociality: The complex biology of hives
chapters.description: Delve into the biological social structures of ants and bees, where individual sacrifice and genetics guarantee the life of the colony.

chapters.title: Adrenaline and the biological escape response
chapters.description: We explore the physiological cascade that floods our body in fractions of a second to instinctively prepare ourselves for a situation of great danger.

chapters.title: Endosymbiotic theory: The complex cellular origin
chapters.description: We analyze the fascinating theory that explains how ancient cells assimilated entire bacteria, creating today's essential mitochondria.

chapters.title: Genetics of left-handed people: Asymmetry in the brain
chapters.description: We discovered the genetic and neurological variables that determine why a minority of the global human population has left-side dominance.

chapters.title: The human liver: Our great biological laboratory
chapters.description: We study the multiple metabolic and detoxifying functions of this surprising internal organ capable of regenerating in an incredible biological way.

chapters.title: Animal Mimicry: The Evolutionary Art of Deception
chapters.description: We observe the extraordinary genetic adaptations that allow certain prey to camouflage themselves perfectly in the environment to evade their predators.

chapters.title: Telomeres and their link to life expectancy
chapters.description: We break down the vital biological function of the ends of our chromosomes and their deep molecular connection to the overall aging process.

chapters.title: Speciation: The biological origin of new species
chapters.description: We understand the evolutionary and genetic mechanisms that divide a single original population until they become reproductively very isolated entities.

chapters.title: Biology of the inner ear and the magic of hearing
chapters.description: We explore the delicate mechanical and neural structures that capture invisible sound waves and transform them into rhythmic signals for the brain.

chapters.title: The biological evolution of human skin color
chapters.description: We look at the evolutionary history of melanin and how our ancestors biologically adapted to the levels of ultraviolet radiation from the sun.

chapters.title: Physiology of the kidneys and our excretory system
chapters.description: We enter the complex filtration system of our vital body, responsible for purifying the blood and regulating the healthy balance of fluids.

chapters.title: The genetic determination of human and animal sex
chapters.description: We understand the role of sex chromosomes in fetal development and discover other amazing biological strategies within the living animal kingdom.

chapters.title: The axolotl and the miracle of cell regeneration
chapters.description: We explore the amazing capacity of this amphibian, capable of regenerating complete limbs, important organs and even fundamental parts of its brain.

chapters.title: Fermentation biology and useful bacteria
chapters.description: We study how certain microorganisms transform food biologically without using oxygen, allowing the ancient creation of bread, cheeses and wines.

chapters.title: Neurobiology of the sense of smell and its receptors
chapters.description: We break down how our neurons manage to identify thousands of molecules in the air to trigger biological responses and deeply vivid memories.

chapters.title: Plant neurobiology and hidden communication
chapters.description: We analyze the scientific evidence on how plants perceive the real environment, warn themselves of serious dangers, and send underground chemical signals.

chapters.title: The genetics of human hair and common baldness
chapters.description: We discover the hereditary and biological basis that determines follicular texture, hair thickness and why so many humans lose all their hair as they grow.

chapters.title: Magnetoreception: The sixth sense of animals
chapters.description: We explore the amazing biological sense that allows migratory birds, turtles and various insects to perceive all of the Earth's magnetic fields.

chapters.title: Endogenous retroviruses: Viral genes in our DNA
chapters.description: We understand the shocking biological discovery that a large part of our human genome comes directly from ancient virological infections.

chapters.title: Biology of the sense of taste and our papillae
chapters.description: We look at the fascinating chemistry that occurs in our tongue to distinguish flavors and how this great biological evolution protected us from deadly poisons.

chapters.title: Gene therapy: The cure by rewriting our DNA
chapters.description: We explore promising medical advances that seek to cure serious inherited diseases through the direct insertion of biologically healthy genes.

chapters.title: Basic Embryology: The Amazing Origin of the Fetus
chapters.description: We walk through the vital stages of human biological development in the womb, observing how a simple group of cells forms such complex organs.

chapters.title: Abyssal gigantism: The biology of the depths
chapters.description: We discover why squid and crustaceans that live in the dark and icy ocean trenches develop such enormous biological sizes.

chapters.title: Marine plankton and its immense ecological impact
chapters.description: We study the essential microorganisms that float freely in our seas, responsible for producing much of the oxygen we breathe today.

chapters.title: Lung physiology: The biology of breathing
chapters.description: We understand the vital gas exchange in our lung alveoli and how oxygen manages to efficiently reach the bloodstream.

chapters.title: Insular dwarfism and the biology of Homo floresiensis
chapters.description: We discovered how resource limitation on isolated islands causes astonishing evolutionary size reductions in various animal species.

chapters.title: Parthenogenesis: Animal reproduction without fertilization
chapters.description: We study the amazing biological phenomenon through which some female reptiles and fish manage to conceive offspring without male material.

chapters.title: The gut-brain axis and its neurobiological impact
chapters.description: We analyze the constant chemical communication between our intestinal bacteria and the central nervous system to regulate mood.

chapters.title: Bone tissue: The dynamic biology of the human skeleton
chapters.description: We explore the living structure of our bones, how they are continually remodeled throughout our lives, and their vital role in metabolic health.

chapters.title: Antibodies: Key proteins in our immunity
chapters.description: We break down the biological structure of immunoglobulins and how they manage to highly specifically identify invading pathogens.

chapters.title: The biology of monotremes and the southern platypus
chapters.description: Delve into the strange evolutionary characteristics of these primitive mammals unique in the biological world that still manage to lay eggs.

chapters.title: Genetic deextinction: Is it possible to revive species?
chapters.description: We examine the great scientific efforts and enormous genetic barriers that exist in trying to bring back the extinct woolly mammoth.

chapters.title: The neurobiology of bodily pain and its vital function
chapters.description: We understand the complex nerve receptors that alert us to damage to biological tissues and why experiencing pain is so crucial.

chapters.title: Genetic drift: The role of chance in real evolution
chapters.description: We study how random fluctuations in biological populations can remove or fix genes independently of adaptation.

chapters.title: The biology of the pancreas and glucose regulation
chapters.description: We analyze the vital cellular production of insulin and glucagon to maintain a perfect balance of available sugar in the bloodstream.

chapters.title: Marsupials and the biological development of their offspring
chapters.description: We discovered how kangaroos and koalas give birth to immature fetuses that must complete their growth inside a specialized biological pouch.

chapters.title: The biology of chronic stress and the hormone cortisol
chapters.description: We explore the serious physiological consequences that our body suffers when the vital adaptive alarm response fails to be deactivated.

chapters.title: Green fluorescent protein and the cellular revolution
chapters.description: Learn how a brilliant biological molecule extracted from jellyfish allowed geneticists to observe complex living processes in real time.

chapters.title: Genetic Ancestry Testing: Reading Our Past
chapters.description: We clarify how laboratories sequence the human genome to try to trace our ancient biological migrations across the globe.

chapters.title: Ribosomes: The biological factory of proteins
chapters.description: We delve into the molecular machinery that is responsible for reading the human genetic code in order to assemble each structural part of the body.

chapters.title: Biological sexual selection and the peacock tail
chapters.description: We analyze the evolutionary forces that drive the development of very extravagant and biologically costly physical traits to attract mates.

chapters.title: The biology of the human voice and vocal cords
chapters.description: We explain the sophisticated anatomical mechanism of our larynx that allows us to articulate complex sounds and develop our unique language.

chapters.title: The biology of human skin: Our largest organ
chapters.description: We study the complex cellular layers of the epidermis and dermis, responsible for physically protecting us and regulating our temperature.

chapters.title: The Denisovans: The other great human branch that became extinct
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating genetic evidence that reveals the ancient existence of these mysterious human relatives on the Asian continent.

chapters.title: Biological neurotoxins: An attack on the nervous system
chapters.description: We understand exactly how the deadly venoms of snakes and scorpions manage to block nerve signals to paralyze their prey.

chapters.title: The biology of sensory synesthesia in humans
chapters.description: We discover the peculiar neurological and biological crossover that causes certain people to be able to taste words, see music or hear colors.

chapters.title: The exoskeleton of arthropods: Biological armor
chapters.description: We analyze the evolutionary advantages of having a chitin shell and the vulnerabilities that insects face during their great life molt.

chapters.title: Cartilage biology and vital joint function
chapters.description: We explore this vital biological tissue that acts as a great shock absorber for our human body, allowing fluid sliding of the bones.

chapters.title: Oxytocin and the complex neurobiology of human attachment
chapters.description: We study this fascinating hormone responsible for strengthening maternal biological bonds, couple relationships and fostering great empathy.

chapters.title: The Golgi apparatus: The great cellular shipping center
chapters.description: We delve into this crucial cellular organelle responsible for modifying, packaging and distributing large proteins throughout our human body.

chapters.title: Our biological clocks and the genetics of the vital rhythm
chapters.description: We analyze how certain genes regulate our daily internal cycles and the enormous physiological challenges that crossing time zones causes us.

chapters.title: The biology of lichens: An extraordinary alliance
chapters.description: We discovered the incredible and resistant symbiosis between a fungus and an algae that gives rise to these biological beings capable of colonizing bare rocks.

chapters.title: Human platelets and the miracle of coagulation
chapters.description: We understand the vital and complex biological mechanism that stops our bleeding and how our blood manages to solidify correctly.

chapters.title: The genetic bottleneck and the risk of extinction
chapters.description: We explain the terrible consequences of the drastic loss of genetic diversity and how it severely affected the cheetahs of today's Africa.

chapters.title: Prions and the incredible infectious proteins
chapters.description: We discovered the strange biology of these misfolded proteins that manage to cause serious neurological diseases without possessing any genetic material.

chapters.title: The Neurobiology of Serotonin and Mood
chapters.description: We explore the fundamental role of this neurotransmitter in the great chemical regulation of our emotions, daily appetite and our mental well-being.

chapters.title: Adaptive radiation: The rapid evolution of species
chapters.description: We analyze how a single ancestral species manages to diversify rapidly into multiple biological forms in order to conquer new ecological niches.

chapters.title: The human spleen: A vital biological organ much ignored
chapters.description: We study the crucial immunological and blood filtration functions that this silent abdominal organ performs to protect the body's health.

chapters.title: Glial cells: The vital biological support of the brain
chapters.description: We delve into the nervous tissue to learn about the companions of neurons, essential for cellular nutrition and the defense of our brain.

chapters.title: Sickle cell anemia and its biological link with malaria
chapters.description: We understand the fascinating evolutionary case of how a genetic mutation that deforms our red blood cells provided biological resistance to malaria.

chapters.title: Plant tropisms: How plants decide to move
chapters.description: We analyze the surprising biological mechanisms by which plants detect light and gravity to guide the growth of all their stems.

chapters.title: The human virome: The thousands of viruses that live in you
chapters.description: We study the enormous population of harmless viral agents that naturally inhabit our bodies and their crucial role in great biological health.

chapters.title: Brown fat: The wonderful thermal biological tissue
chapters.description: We discovered the physiological function of this cellular tissue that burns metabolic energy quickly to maintain the vital heat of all mammals.

chapters.title: Epigenetic inheritance: The biological impact of trauma
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating genetic evidence suggesting that traumatic experiences can alter DNA expression and be passed on to future generations.

chapters.title: Mycorrhizal networks: The underground biological internet
chapters.description: We delve into the amazing biological and symbiotic association between forest trees and fungi that allows them to share nutrients and chemical signals.

chapters.title: Biomimetics: Human technology inspired by life
chapters.description: We analyze how modern scientists manage to copy the brilliant evolutionary designs of our living nature to create very amazing technologies.

chapters.title: Cilia and flagella: The small motors of cells
chapters.description: We understand the complex and efficient microscopic structure that allows many cells to propel themselves vigorously and swim in biological fluids.

chapters.title: The pineal gland and the biology of our melatonin
chapters.description: We study how this small organ of the brain responds to natural light to regulate our biological rhythm and allow us to achieve a great night's rest.

chapters.title: Coral bleaching and its great ecological crisis
chapters.description: We explain the biology of ocean heat stress that causes the deadly symbiotic breakdown between wonderful marine corals and their vital nutritional algae.

chapters.title: Endocrine disruptors: A biological and environmental challenge
chapters.description: We explore how certain modern chemicals manage to mimic our natural hormones, confusing our body's vital signals and health.

chapters.title: Biology of the placenta: The disposable human organ
chapters.description: We analyze the development of this amazing temporary biological tissue that nourishes the fetus and also defends it immunologically throughout gestation.

chapters.title: Biological endorphins and great pain relief
chapters.description: We discover the neurobiology of these natural painkillers that our body produces in order to mitigate physical suffering and provide us with deep calm.

chapters.title: Animal hermaphroditism: Biology of the united sexes
chapters.description: We study the extraordinary species of our planet that biologically developed male and female reproductive systems in the same organism.

chapters.title: Plant senescence: The biology of autumn leaves
chapters.description: We understand the orderly cellular and chemical process by which our deciduous trees decide to reabsorb their nutrients before releasing each leaf.

chapters.title: The biology of addiction and neurological hijacking
chapters.description: We analyze the strong neurochemical alterations that substances generate in our biological reward circuits to create great chronic dependence.

chapters.title: The human appendix: A useless biological organ or not?
chapters.description: We demystify the biology of this intestinal appendage by reviewing new evidence that considers it a vital biological refuge for beneficial bacteria.

chapters.title: Animal biofluorescence: The secret light of ecology
chapters.description: We explain the biological difference between chemically producing light and the amazing cellular ability to absorb photons to glow in the oceanic darkness.

chapters.title: Macrophages and the vital human cleanup squad
chapters.description: We enter the cellular battlefront to learn about the large white blood cells specialized in engulfing pathogens and biological remains.

chapters.title: Biology of spiciness: Why are we fascinated by this heat?
chapters.description: We discovered how capsaicin chemically tricks our body's thermal receptors, causing that illusion of great physical heat that fascinates many.

chapters.title: Hox Genes: The Biological Architects of the Human Body
chapters.description: We study this fascinating group of genes that regulate enormous embryonic development and dictate exactly where each limb should go in living beings.

chapters.title: HeLa cells: The biology of the strange immortal lineage
chapters.description: We analyze the historic biological case of human oncological cells that never stop dividing and that completely revolutionized genetic science.

chapters.title: Chemical communication: The biological language of ants
chapters.description: We explore the sophisticated pheromones and olfactory signals that these complex social insects use to biologically organize their entire daily colony.

chapters.title: T lymphocytes: The special forces of the immune system
chapters.description: We understand how these complex cells mature in our human thymus to destroy hidden viral threats in a highly specialized way.

chapters.title: Phantom limb: The neurobiology of a grand illusion
chapters.description: We explore the mysterious and complex brain phenomenon by which many patients perceive physical sensations in their limbs that have been amputated.

chapters.title: Tardigrades and extreme biological resistance
chapters.description: Discover the molecular secrets of water bears, microscopic beings capable of surviving the vacuum of space and lethal radiation.

chapters.title: The biology of sweat and temperature control
chapters.description: We analyze the crucial physiological mechanism of human perspiration and how our glands protect us from suffering serious heat stroke.

chapters.title: Color blindness: The genetics behind visual perception
chapters.description: We understand inherited mutations in our retinal photoreceptors that alter the biological ability to distinguish colors.

chapters.title: Lethal Phytochemistry: The Biology of Toxic Plants
chapters.description: We explore the complex defensive molecules that evolutionary botany has developed to intoxicate and paralyze their large predators.

chapters.title: Bioelectrogenesis: Animals that generate electricity
chapters.description: We discovered the cellular anatomy of marine eels and rays capable of emitting strong electrical discharges to hunt and defend themselves.

chapters.title: DNA Repair: Our Genetic Control System
chapters.description: We study the diligent enzyme complexes responsible for daily reviewing and correcting the lethal errors in our biological code.

chapters.title: The gallbladder and the chemical biology of digestion
chapters.description: We analyze the functioning of the small organ that stores liver bile, a biological substance vital to breaking down the fats we ingest.

chapters.title: Lysosomes: The recycling system of the large cell
chapters.description: We immerse ourselves in cellular vesicles filled with powerful enzymes that break down biological waste to maintain a very clean interior.

chapters.title: The neurobiology of octopuses and their great intelligence
chapters.description: We explore the fascinating distributed brain of these marine invertebrates, their incredible cognitive and problem-solving abilities.

chapters.title: Dendrochronology: The biology of tree growth
chapters.description: We understand how plant cambium forms annual concentric rings that reveal the climatic history and vital wounds of forests.

chapters.title: Viral latency: The viruses that sleep in our DNA
chapters.description: We analyze the insidious strategies of herpes and human HIV to hide silently in cells and reactivate with great force.

chapters.title: Key species: The great architects of the ecosystem
chapters.description: We discover why the biological disappearance of wolves and beavers can lead to the sudden collapse of vast, intricate natural environments.

chapters.title: Genetic epistasis: When genes silence others
chapters.description: We study the amazing and complex biological interaction where a specific gene manages to block the visible expression of other DNA sequences.

chapters.title: Olfactory fatigue: Why we stop perceiving odors
chapters.description: We understand the neurobiological mechanism by which our nasal receptors quickly become saturated and cancel daily aromatic stimuli.

chapters.title: Hydrothermal vents: The dark biology of the deep sea
chapters.description: We explore the extraordinary ecosystems that thrive in the ocean darkness, biologically sustained by vital chemosynthetic bacteria.

chapters.title: The biology of fever: Heat to kill pathogens
chapters.description: We analyze the crucial response of the human hypothalamus to raise our internal temperature and create a hostile environment for biological infections.

chapters.title: Photoperiodicity: How do plants measure time?
chapters.description: We discovered the impressive botanical photoreceptors that measure the exact hours of light and darkness to trigger the vital flowering season.

chapters.title: Frozen Frogs: The Biology of Cryopreservation
chapters.description: We study the incredible biological amphibians that manage to survive extreme winter by making an amazing internal cellular antifreeze.

chapters.title: Genetic chimerism: Two different DNAs in a single body
chapters.description: We understand the shocking biological phenomenon where two embryonic twins fuse early, creating adults with double genetic sequences.

chapters.title: Mirror neurons: The neurobiology of great empathy
chapters.description: We analyze the fascinating brain cells that are biologically activated both when we act and when we observe the actions of other beings.

chapters.title: Plant allelopathy: The incessant botanical chemical war
chapters.description: We discovered how certain botanical species release powerful natural toxins into the soil to actively inhibit the growth of rival plants.

chapters.title: Biological pleiotropy: A single gene for many traits
chapters.description: We explore the intriguing hereditary phenomenon where a single gene in our sequence manages to affect completely unrelated body characteristics.

chapters.title: The biology of spiders and the resistance of their great silk

chapters.description: We explore the valuable ecosystem strongly dominated by lactic bacteria that maintain the pH balance to avoid serious internal infections.

chapters.title: Botanical photoreceptors and algal phototaxis
chapters.description: We analyze how simple single-celled plant organisms manage to move skillfully in search of large light sources to maximize their photosynthesis.

chapters.title: Genetic biomarkers in today's major diagnosis
chapters.description: We study the amazing use of the unique molecular signatures of our valuable DNA to detect complex diseases at very early stages today.

chapters.title: Osmotic regulation of salmon in their great migration
chapters.description: We discovered the extreme and rapid physiological adjustment in the large gills of these wonderful fish when moving from salty marine water to cold rivers.

chapters.title: The major human histocompatibility complex
chapters.description: We delve into the wonderful proteins that fragments of invading viruses display to immediately alert the body's large lymphocytes.

chapters.title: Genetics of aggressiveness and our strong behavior
chapters.description: We understand the immense neurobiological influence that certain mutations in strong enzymatic genes exert on major human anger attacks.

chapters.title: Bioluminescent organs of the immense abyssal fauna
chapters.description: We explore the precise cellular chemical structure of large photophores and the amazing symbiotic union that illuminates the terrible darkness.

chapters.title: Hematopoietic stem cells and the great renewal
chapters.description: We analyze the infinite and valuable production of large red blood cells daily and how transplants manage to cure lethal diseases of our blood.

chapters.title: The great biology of ape vocalizations
chapters.description: We study the enormous laryngeal structures that allow these fascinating and strong evolutionary relatives to communicate over long distances in the jungle.

chapters.title: Bowman's glands and our olfactory mucosa
chapters.description: We discovered the valuable mucus solvent that dissolves the invisible chemical molecules of the immense air to allow direct contact with each neuron.

chapters.title: Convergent evolution of spines in plants
chapters.description: We explore how different huge isolated botanical families genetically developed formidable, sharp leaves to repel large herbivores.

chapters.title: Inhibitory neurotransmitters and gamma acid today
chapters.description: We understand the immense calming impact of the wonderful biological molecule GABA to curb extreme neuronal excitation and prevent lethal anxiety.

chapters.title: Pollination by bats in the warm tropics
chapters.description: We analyze the wonderful botanical adaptations where large nocturnal flowers provide abundant vital nectar in exchange for great pollen transport.

chapters.title: Cellular epigenomics and developmental programming
chapters.description: We study how the immense pattern of cellular chemical marks is masterfully established during the vital embryonic phase of each large human tissue.

chapters.title: The cell biology of alveolar macrophages today
chapters.description: We delve into the immense pulmonary system to meet the great dust devourers that constantly sweep our airways today.

chapters.title: Sequential hermaphroditism in large aquatic fauna
chapters.description: We discovered the strong social signals and vital hormones that command many amazing marine fish to change their natural sex throughout their lives.

chapters.title: Physiology and biology of the immense vocal cords
chapters.description: We explore the immense elasticity of the wonderful muscular bands of the larynx that allow us to emit high and very deep bass tones.

chapters.title: Sympatric speciation in the same wild ecosystem
chapters.description: We understand the astonishing biological reproductive isolation that occurs when valuable organisms decide to exploit large niches without physical separation.

chapters.title: The eternal life of the amazing Turritopsis polyp today
chapters.description: We analyze the incredible genetic mystery of the amazing biological jellyfish that reverts its powerful mature cells to a great vital juvenile state.
